metadata.research_scope: Comprehensive analysis of open-source and self-hosted AI tools for image generation, video generation, image editing, multi-agent frameworks, and self-hosted APIs
metadata.note: All complexity ratings are on a 1-10 scale (1=trivial, 10=extremely complex). Setup times are estimated based on prior experience with similar tools.

stability_ai_stable_diffusion.name: Stability AI - Stable Diffusion 3.5
stability_ai_stable_diffusion.description: Open-source image generation models with three variants: Large, Large Turbo (4-step fast), and Medium (2.6B params for consumer hardware)
key_features: Multiple size options for different hardware

hugging_face_diffusers.name: Hugging Face Diffusers
hugging_face_diffusers.description: Unified library for state-of-the-art diffusion models supporting text-to-image, image-to-image, inpainting, and outpainting

automatic1111_webui.name: AUTOMATIC1111 Stable Diffusion WebUI
automatic1111_webui.description: Feature-rich web interface for Stable Diffusion with extensive customization, extensions, and community support
automatic1111_webui.hardware_requirements: NVIDIA GPU with CUDA support, 8GB+ VRAM recommended
automatic1111_webui.setup_notes: Installation is quick but has strict Python version requirements. Subsequent launches start instantly.

comfyui.name: ComfyUI
comfyui.description: Node-based, modular approach to image generation workflows with full control and flexibility
dependencies: ComfyUI Manager (optional, simplifies custom node installation)
comfyui.learning_curve: Steep but rewarding for advanced users who want precise control

localai.name: LocalAI
localai.description: Drop-in OpenAI API replacement supporting image generation alongside LLMs and audio
key_features: Image generation, LLM, audio in single API

animatediff.name: AnimateDiff
animatediff.description: Plug-and-play motion module that turns Stable Diffusion into video generators without retraining
animatediff.hardware_requirements: Strong NVIDIA GPU, 8GB+ VRAM minimum, 24GB+ recommended

stable_video_diffusion.name: Stable Video Diffusion
stable_video_diffusion.description: Open-source video generation model from Stability AI with both image-to-video and text-to-video capabilities

mochi_1.name: Mochi 1
mochi_1.description: Open-source 10B parameter video generation model from Genmo AI with smooth, realistic 30 fps output
mochi_1.hardware_requirements: Very demanding - 24GB+ VRAM, high-end NVIDIA GPU required

ebsynth.name: EbSynth / ReEzSynth (Open Source)
ebsynth.description: Video style transfer and frame-based synthesis without requiring generative models. Transforms video based on keyframe edits.
ebsynth.pricing: Free/Open Source (EbSynth app is closed-source but free)
key_features: Paint one frame to transform entire video
ebsynth.hardware_requirements: 4GB+ VRAM GPU, CPU option available but slower
ebsynth.use_cases: Video stylization, animation conversion, colorization, digital makeup

openai_sora.name: OpenAI Sora
openai_sora.description: CLOSED SOURCE - Cutting-edge text-to-video model. Mentioned for comparison only.
openai_sora.note: Closed source, only available via paid API

opencv.name: OpenCV (cv2)
opencv.description: Industrial-strength computer vision library with extensive image processing capabilities

pillow.name: Pillow (PIL Fork)
pillow.description: Python Imaging Library fork - simple, user-friendly image processing for common tasks
pillow.best_for: Quick image processing, file conversion, basic manipulation

rembg.name: RemBG
rembg.description: AI-powered background removal using ONNX models without heavy dependencies
dependencies: Optional: onnxruntime-gpu, CUDA, cuDNN for GPU support
rembg.hardware_requirements: 2GB+ VRAM for GPU, CPU option available

gfpgan.name: GFPGAN
gfpgan.description: Generative Facial Prior for blind face restoration and enhancement
key_features: Blind face enhancement (no need for paired training data)
Compatible with Real-ESRGAN for background enhancement
gfpgan.use_cases: Old photo restoration, face enhancement, quality improvement

langchain.name: LangChain
langchain.description: Agent engineering platform for building multi-agent systems with LangGraph specialization
langchain.growth: 220% increase in GitHub stars from Q1 2024-Q1 2025

crewai.name: CrewAI
crewai.description: Lean, independent multi-agent orchestration framework optimized for role-based autonomous agents
crewai.differentiators: Dual approach: Crews for adaptive collaboration, Flows for deterministic control

autogen.name: AutoGen (Microsoft) - Legacy
autogen.description: Multi-agent conversation framework. NOW IN MAINTENANCE MODE - Microsoft recommends new Agent Framework instead.
autogen.warning: MAINTENANCE MODE - Use Microsoft Agent Framework for new projects

pydantic_ai.name: Pydantic AI
pydantic_ai.description: Type-safe agent framework leveraging Pydantic's validation with structured outputs
pydantic_ai.best_for: Type-safe applications where data validation is critical

llamaindex.name: LlamaIndex
llamaindex.description: Framework for building context-aware agent systems with focus on data indexing and retrieval
llamaindex.learning_curve: Medium - powerful but requires understanding of RAG patterns

ollama.name: Ollama
ollama.description: Docker-like simplicity for running open-source LLMs locally with OpenAI API compatibility
key_features: Multiple model support (Llama, Mistral, Gemma, etc.)
ollama.hardware_requirements: 8GB RAM minimum (Llama 3.2 3B), 40GB+ for 70B models, GPU optional
ollama.quick_start: ollama pull llama2 && ollama run llama2
ollama.use_cases: Local development, privacy-focused applications, offline inference

vllm.name: vLLM
vllm.description: High-throughput, memory-efficient inference and serving engine for LLMs with tensor parallelism
key_features: High throughput (10-40x faster than standard serving)
vllm.supported_accelerators: NVIDIA, AMD (ROCm), Intel, Apple Silicon, Google TPU, IBM Spyre, Huawei Ascend
vllm.best_for: Production inference servers needing high throughput and optimization

text_generation_inference.name: Text Generation Inference (TGI)
text_generation_inference.description: Hugging Face's production-ready inference server with OpenAI API compatibility
text_generation_inference.supported_models: Llama, Falcon, StarCoder, BLOOM, GPT-NeoX, T5

ray_serve.name: Ray Serve
ray_serve.description: Scalable model serving framework for distributed inference with multi-model and multi-GPU support
ray_serve.pricing: Free/Open Source (Ray is hosted via Anyscale for enterprise)
ray_serve.hardware_requirements: CPU capable, GPU optional for acceleration, works on single or multi-node clusters
ray_serve.learning_curve: Steep - requires understanding of Ray actors and distributed computing
ray_serve.best_for: Complex production systems requiring multi-model orchestration, auto-scaling, and advanced scheduling

quality_vs_speed.most_flexible: Stable Diffusion 3.5 Medium (runs on consumer hardware)

image_generation_rankings.recommendation: Start with Hugging Face Diffusers for learning, AUTOMATIC1111 for user-friendly production, ComfyUI for advanced control

video_generation_status.best_option: AnimateDiff for image-to-video, Mochi 1 for quality
video_generation_status.not_recommended: Closed-source Haiper AI (shutdown in Feb 2025), use alternatives instead

image_editing_workflow.simple_tasks: Pillow - file conversion, resize, basic filters
image_editing_workflow.integration: Use all three together in pipelines

multi_agent_selection.type_safe: Pydantic AI - best validation and type safety
multi_agent_selection.avoid: AutoGen - in maintenance mode, use Microsoft Agent Framework instead

self_hosted_api.production_ready: Text Generation Inference (TGI) - proven at scale

free_open_source: All image generation models (Stable Diffusion, Diffusers, AUTOMATIC1111, ComfyUI)
All video generation models (AnimateDiff, Stable Video Diffusion, Mochi 1, EbSynth)
All multi-agent frameworks except LangSmith observability
All self-hosted APIs (Ollama, vLLM, TGI, Ray Serve)
optional_paid: Hugging Face Inference API (optional hosted option)

hardware_costs.budget_cpu_option: CPU-only inference possible but much slower

image_generation.alternative: Hugging Face Diffusers - lighter weight, more control
image_generation.integration: Use with LocalAI if you need unified API for multiple modalities

video_generation.status: Research phase - not production ready yet
video_generation.recommended_approach: AnimateDiff for image-to-video pipeline (can create videos from generated images)

image_editing.general_processing: Pillow for common tasks, OpenCV for advanced

orchestration.primary: CrewAI - most intuitive for multi-agent workflows
orchestration.secondary: Pydantic AI - if type safety is critical

api_serving.development: Ollama - simplest to set up and use
api_serving.complex_needs: Ray Serve for multi-model complex orchestration
